blocks.text: Dear Mr. Palmer:

This letter sets out the terms on which we will represent {{fact:f:E-2021-001.client}} in patent prosecution support for the Keith-Duarte portfolio. Please read it in full before you sign it. If a term here does not match your understanding of what we discussed, mark it and send it back marked. I would rather revise a letter than argue about one.
Scope of the Engagement

We will act as counsel of record for the applications in the families you identify to us in writing, and for the prosecution work those families generate before the United States Patent and Trademark Office. The work includes intake and review of the transferred files, docketing of every date those files carry, preparation and filing of responses to office actions and to restriction and election requirements, information disclosure statements and the related formalities, and counsel on continuation, divisional, and abandonment decisions as they arise.

The engagement does not include litigation or contested post-grant proceedings, foreign filing beyond instructions to associates you have already engaged, opinions of counsel on infringement or validity, or freedom-to-operate analysis. Each of those is a separate undertaking and takes a separate letter. Scope is what this section says it is, not what the subject matter suggests. If you need something that is not here, ask, and we will amend.
Approach

Every matter is docketed on intake and confirmed back to you in writing, with the dates we calculated and the papers we calculated them from. Where a deadline can be extended, we will give you the unextended date, the extended date, and the cost of the extension before the earlier one passes. We will not let an unextended date go by on the assumption that an extension will be purchased later. If a file arrives incomplete, we docket to the papers we have and tell you the same week what is missing.

You will have our conclusions in writing. Views offered on a call about how a family looks are working discussion, are not an opinion, and should not be relied on or repeated as one. That is not caution for its own sake. A characterization of scope or validity that travels outside a written analysis is a document we never got the chance to qualify.
The Team

Heather Munoz, Partner, has overall responsibility for the engagement and is your principal contact. Kelly Snyder, Associate, will take the file histories and the prior-art review; if you receive a question about a sentence an examiner wrote years ago, it will come from her, and it will be worth answering. Joseph Walker, Associate, carries the running docket and the correspondence and will be the name on most of what reaches you. I supervise the engagement and sign anything that commits the firm.
Staffing may change as the work requires. Heather Munoz will tell you before it does, and no one will appear on your matters without her knowing why.
Fees and Payment

Our fee for the work described above is {{fact:f:E-2021-001.fee}}, and the engagement begins {{fact:f:E-2021-001.start}}. Official fees, search costs, and foreign associate charges are billed as incurred and are not covered by that figure. We invoice monthly, and invoices are payable within thirty days of presentation. If you dispute a charge, tell us inside that period and we will hold it out of the balance until it is resolved.

If an invoice goes unpaid, we will tell you once, plainly. If it stays unpaid, we will stop new work and move to withdraw, subject to our obligations to the Office and to you on any matter with a date pending. Withdrawal does not forgive fees earned or costs advanced before it. I put this in the letter rather than into a later conversation, because the later conversation is worse.
Term and Termination

The engagement runs from the start date stated above until the work described is complete, unless one of us ends it sooner. You may end it at any time, for any reason, on written notice. We may end it for non-payment, for a conflict that develops and cannot be waived, or where continuing would require us to act against our professional obligations. On termination we return the files and deliver a docket of every open date, and we state in writing the date on which responsibility for those dates passes to you or to successor counsel. There will be no interval in which each of us assumes the other is watching.
Confidentiality

What you tell us in the course of the representation is confidential, and communications made for the purpose of legal advice are privileged. We will not disclose your information without your consent except where a rule, an order, or our duty of candor to the Office requires it, and where that happens we will tell you what was disclosed and why. Technical disclosures from your engineers are treated the same way. The privilege is yours, not ours. We will not waive it for you, and we will flag anything that puts it at risk.
Conflicts

We have run this matter against our current and former clients and have identified no conflict. We are a boutique and our clients work in overlapping fields, so a conflict may develop later. If one does, we will tell you as soon as we know, and we will not take a position adverse to you in the subject matter of this engagement. Whether we can continue in that circumstance may depend on a waiver we ask you for in writing, and you are free to decline it.
Limitation of Liability

Our aggregate liability arising out of this engagement is limited to the fees paid to us under it, except where such a limit cannot be imposed under the rules of professional conduct or applicable law. Nothing here limits liability for fraud or willful misconduct, waives any right you have to raise a matter with a disciplinary authority, or relieves us of the obligation to tell you when we have made an error. We carry professional liability insurance and will furnish the certificate on request. You should know that this paragraph is a term you are free to negotiate. Some clients do.
Governing Law

This letter is governed by the law of the state in which our office sits and by the rules of professional conduct in force there. Practice before the Office is additionally governed by the applicable federal statutes and regulations, and where those conflict with this letter, they control. Any dispute under this letter will be brought in the courts of that state, and each of us bears its own costs unless a court orders otherwise.

If the foregoing states our agreement, please sign in the space below and return one copy to me. We will not open a matter or docket a date under this letter until the signed copy is back, and until the dates are docketed they are yours to watch rather than ours. That is the only urgency in this letter, and it is a real one. We are glad to be doing this work for Keith-Duarte, and we will do it precisely.

To: Kelly Snyder, Joseph Walker
From: Heather Munoz
Re: Prosecution Support for Keith-Duarte -- kickoff, workstreams, and what I want first
Privileged and confidential. Attorney work product.

The file opened {{fact:f:E-2021-001.start}} and the first tranche of papers from {{fact:f:E-2021-001.client}} is in. Our objective for the opening weeks is narrow, and I want it kept narrow: get every date in the transferred portfolio docketed, confirmed against the papers it came from, and reconciled with what the prior firm recorded. Nothing else in this memo matters until that is done. We do not yet know this portfolio well enough to have a strategy, and I would rather say so than improvise one.

Joseph Palmer is our client contact and expects to hear from us on a cadence rather than by exception. He is a general manager, not a patent lawyer. Write to him accordingly, which means the answer first and the reasoning after, and it does not mean leaving the reasoning out.
Workstreams

items: Intake and docketing (Joseph Walker). Every matter Keith-Duarte transfers gets docketed on receipt against the papers in the wrapper, not against the prior firm's docket sheet. Where the two disagree, ours governs and theirs is a question. Bring me any disagreement the day you find it.

File history review (Kelly Snyder). Read the prosecution history of each family before you read the pending claims. I want to know what was argued, what was disclaimed, and what was left on the table, family by family. Do not summarize the claims for me yet; the claims will read differently once you know what was said to get them.

Formalities (Kelly Snyder, with Joseph Walker). Confirm powers of attorney, correspondence addresses, and assignment recordation for every matter in scope. Inventory the references we have been given against what has actually been submitted. This is the dull work that keeps the interesting work from becoming a problem.

Correspondence and cadence (Joseph Walker). Draft the confirmation schedule listing every docketed date and the paper each was calculated from. Client updates go out against a docket number and stay short.

Scope watch (all). This engagement covers prosecution. It does not cover opinions, freedom-to-operate work, or anything adjacent that arrives as a question on a phone call. Route those to me and do not answer them helpfully. An answer given in passing is still an answer, and it will be quoted back without the sentence you would have put around it.

blocks.text: Next Steps

items: Joseph Walker: confirmation schedule to me this week, with the docket reconciled against the transferred files before it goes anywhere.
Kelly Snyder: the first two families read, and a memorandum on what those histories constrain. State what you searched and what you did not, in the memorandum, not in a footnote.
Heather Munoz: call with Joseph Palmer to set the reporting cadence and to tell him what we found in the transferred docket, whether or not it is a comfortable call.

All: nothing goes to {{fact:f:E-2021-001.client}} during intake that I have not read first. This is not a comment on your drafting. It is that a client forms its view of a firm in the first month, and I would rather that view be formed on purpose.
All: log your time daily rather than weekly. The fee on this engagement was quoted against an assumption about the transferred files, and I want to know early if the assumption was wrong.

blocks.text: One more thing, and it is the only part of this memo I will not negotiate. We took this file with dates already running on it. If any of you finds a date closer than it should be, you tell me the hour you find it, not at the next meeting and not once you have worked out how it happened. How it happened is a conversation we can have afterward, and we will have it without blame. The date is not a conversation. It is a date.
Joseph Palmer -- copying Kelly Snyder and Joseph Walker.

Intake is closed. Every matter in the Keith-Duarte portfolio that {{fact:f:E-2021-001.client}} transferred to us is now docketed against the papers in its own wrapper, and the confirmation schedule went to you last week listing each date and what we calculated it from. Two of our dates fall earlier than the ones on the transferred sheet. We are working to ours. If prior counsel disagrees, we would like the basis this month rather than nearer the date.

Kelly Snyder is through the file histories on the first two families and is drafting the memorandum on what the earlier arguments constrain. It will be longer than you want and shorter than the record deserves. Joseph Walker has the office action due in this cycle; you will get the draft response with a short note on the one election question we think is genuinely open, and it is open, not rhetorical.

One ask. We still do not have assignment recordation confirmed for two of the transferred matters. We would rather resolve that now than find it in a diligence request later, when it stops being paperwork and starts being a question about title. If your records show the recordation was made, send us the reel and frame and we will stop asking. If they do not, tell us that instead and we will handle it; an unrecorded assignment is a small problem this month and a larger one at any point after.

Heather Munoz
Partner
Working session, Prosecution Support for Keith-Duarte. Minutes taken by Joseph Walker. Privileged and confidential.
Attendees

items: Heather Munoz, Partner, Prosecution
Kelly Snyder, Associate, Prosecution
Joseph Walker, Associate, Prosecution
Joseph Palmer, General Manager, Keith-Duarte

blocks.text: Discussion

Heather Munoz opened with the docket, as she has opened every session on this file. All live dates across the portfolio are scheduled with time in hand and none sits inside thirty days. Two dates were re-derived from the wrappers during intake and remain earlier than the dates the prior firm recorded. Joseph Palmer asked whether the discrepancy had been resolved with prior counsel. It has not; they have not answered our letter. Heather Munoz said the firm works to its own dates whether or not it is resolved, and that resolving it is a courtesy to the record rather than a condition of the docket.

Kelly Snyder walked her file history memorandum on the two lead families. Her conclusion, stated with the limits she put on it: the arguments made in the parent applications constrain the claim scope available in the pending continuations more than the transfer summary from prior counsel indicated. She was explicit about what she did not search, which is anything outside the wrappers as received and any foreign counterpart. Joseph Palmer asked whether that meant the portfolio was weaker than he had been told. Heather Munoz declined to put it that way and said the memorandum says what it says; a claim can be narrower than hoped and still be worth prosecuting.

The restriction requirement in the pending device application took most of the hour. Joe walked through the two groups on offer and what electing each one costs. Heather Munoz asked what the claims would have to say for the answer to change. Joe's answer was that if the linking claim survives, the question is moot; if it does not, electing the apparatus group leaves the method claims to a divisional that {{fact:f:E-2021-001.client}} would have to fund. Joseph Palmer asked for the cost of that divisional before he decides, and asked that it arrive as a figure rather than as a range. Joseph Walker agreed to that.

Assignment recordation on the two outstanding matters is closed. Joseph Palmer's team located the recordation and sent the reel and frame; Kelly Snyder confirmed both against the Office's records rather than against the copies provided, which is the only confirmation worth having. Powers of attorney and correspondence addresses were checked in the same pass and are correct across every matter in scope.

On reporting, Joseph Palmer asked whether the monthly report could carry the docket sheet itself rather than a summary of it. Heather Munoz agreed, on one condition: the sheet carries the unextended and the extended date for every matter in separate columns, so that no one reading it mistakes an extension we have not bought for time we already have.
Action Items

rows: Revised docket sheet with unextended and extended dates in separate columns
Cost of the divisional, stated as a figure and not a range
Memorandum on the linking claim and its effect on the election
Election decision confirmed to the firm in writing
Second letter to prior counsel on the date discrepancy

blocks.text: Prepared by Heather Munoz, Partner. This report covers the current reporting period on the Keith-Duarte prosecution engagement. Privileged and confidential; prepared for the client and its counsel.
Status Summary

The engagement is on schedule and at roughly its midpoint. Intake, docketing, and reconciliation of the transferred portfolio are complete and behind us, and the substantive prosecution work is now the whole of the effort. No date has been missed and none is presently at risk. Measured against the engagement fee of {{fact:f:E-2021-001.fee}}, our time to date sits somewhat under half. That is where it should be at this point, and it is not a prediction that the second half will cost less than the first. Two items below could move it. Both are flagged here rather than absorbed quietly.
Accomplishments This Period

The office action responses due in this cycle were prepared, reviewed, and filed. Joseph Walker drafted them and I reviewed them. Neither required an extension and neither used the full response period. That is deliberate. We do not treat an extension fee as a budget line. We treat it as evidence that something went wrong upstream of the deadline.

Kelly Snyder completed the file history review of the two lead families and delivered her memorandum. I will state her finding as she stated it rather than smoothing it: the prosecution arguments made in the parent applications constrain the claim scope available in the pending continuations more than the transfer summary from prior counsel indicated. The memorandum sets out what she searched and what she did not. It is privileged. It should stay inside {{fact:f:E-2021-001.client}} and its counsel, and it should not be forwarded to a customer, an investor, or a prospective acquirer without speaking to me first.

The restriction requirement in the pending device application was answered. We elected the apparatus group, on your written instruction and after the cost of the divisional was put in front of you as a figure. The method claims are preserved for a divisional and are docketed as such. Nothing about that election is irreversible on the current record.

Assignment recordation for the two matters that transferred with gaps has been confirmed against the Office's records. Powers of attorney and correspondence addresses are now correct across every matter in scope. This is unglamorous work and it is the reason nothing has surprised us so far.
Upcoming Work

The remainder of the engagement is heavier in substance and lighter in administration than the part behind us. We expect responses in the remaining families, a continuation decision on the lead family that will need an answer from you rather than from us, and the divisional filing if you elect to make it. Kelly Snyder will extend the file history review to the families not yet covered. Joseph Walker continues to carry the docket and the correspondence, and the revised docket sheet, with the unextended and extended dates in separate columns, goes out with the next report as requested.

We will also need a decision on the two matters where the claims as pending are, in our current view, narrower than the products they were drafted to cover. That is a business question about whether to spend prosecution effort on scope you may not obtain, and it is yours to answer. We will give you the analysis. We will not make the call, and we would be doing you no favor by pretending the answer is obvious.
Risks

items: The date discrepancy with prior counsel remains unresolved. We have written twice and have had no substantive response. Our docket runs to our own dates, so the exposure is small, but the discrepancy sits on the record and we would prefer it closed rather than explained later.

The scope finding in the lead family may reduce what the portfolio is worth in a licensing conversation. That is a conclusion about claim scope, not about the technology, and it is not final; the continuation strategy exists precisely to test it. We raise it now because you should not learn it from a diligence request.

Budget. The divisional, if elected, and a second round of office actions in the remaining families sit outside the level of effort the fee assumed. We will tell you before we cross that line rather than after. Nothing in this report is a request to change the fee.

blocks.text: Our view of the engagement has not changed since kickoff. The portfolio is in better administrative order than it was, and the substantive questions are now visible rather than buried in a wrapper. Neither of those is a claim about outcome, and neither should be read as one. If you want any part of this report expanded, or want the underlying memoranda rather than my summary of them, ask me and they will come with the qualifications attached.
Closing working session, Prosecution Support for Keith-Duarte. Minutes taken by Joseph Walker. Privileged and confidential.
Attendees

items: Heather Munoz, Partner, Prosecution
Kelly Snyder, Associate, Prosecution
Joseph Walker, Associate, Prosecution
Joseph Palmer, General Manager, Keith-Duarte

blocks.text: Discussion

Heather Munoz opened by reading the docket, which is how every session on this file has opened and how this one closed. Every date that arrived with the transferred portfolio, and every date the portfolio has generated since, has been answered or is scheduled with time in hand. None was missed. No extension was purchased to cover an oversight. Joseph Palmer asked whether that was normal. Heather Munoz said it is the minimum.

Filings this period. The divisional on the method claims was filed and the filing receipt is in the file. Responses went out in the remaining families under prosecution and none required an extension. Kelly Snyder's file history review now covers every family in scope; the memoranda are indexed by family rather than by date, which is how they will be useful to whoever reads them next, including us.

Transition. The engagement covers the work described in the engagement letter and that work is substantially done. Joseph Walker will deliver the closing docket: every open date, the paper each was calculated from, the unextended and extended columns, and separately the matters where what is owed is a decision rather than a filing. Heather Munoz said the firm will state in writing the date on which responsibility for those dates transfers, and that {{fact:f:E-2021-001.client}} should not treat the docket as watched by us after it. Joseph Palmer asked whether follow-on work would need a new letter. It would.

Open decisions. The two families carrying claims narrower than the products they were drafted to cover are still open. Kelly Snyder set out the options and what each one buys, in order of cost. Heather Munoz declined to recommend one, on the ground that the question is what the portfolio is for, and the firm does not know that better than the client does. Joseph Palmer will take it internally. No docket date turns on the answer, which is the only reason it can wait.

Joseph Palmer asked for the firm's candid assessment of the portfolio. Heather Munoz gave it narrowly: the administrative state is sound, the scope questions are documented rather than buried, and nothing in the file history is left for a stranger to find first. She would not go further than that, and said that she would not. Joseph Palmer observed that he had expected a more encouraging answer. Heather Munoz said an encouraging answer is available from anyone and is worth what it costs.

Kelly Snyder raised one item not on the agenda. The continuation in the lead family carries a date that falls after the engagement's work is delivered, and she wanted it said aloud in front of the client rather than recorded in a schedule and assumed. It appears on the closing docket, flagged as a decision owed rather than a filing scheduled. Heather Munoz said that is exactly the kind of date a portfolio loses in a transition, and that the firm would rather be tedious about it now.
Action Items

rows: Closing docket: open dates, source papers, unextended and extended columns
File history memoranda indexed by family and delivered as a set
Written notice of the date responsibility transfers
Decision on the two narrow families
Internal; no docket date depends on it
Final invoice and reconciliation of official fees advanced
After the closing docket is delivered
